content.problemStatement:
For a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of the integers 111 to NNN, and a parameter KKK, we define its winner, denoted win(P,K)\text{win}(P, K)win(P,K), as the result of the following process:


Let x=1x = 1x=1 initially.
For each index iii from 222 to NNN, in order:

If Pi+K‚Č•PxP_i + K \geq P_xPi‚Äč+K‚Č•Px‚Äč, set xxx equal to iii.


win(P,K)\text{win}(P, K)win(P,K) is then defined to be the final value of xxx, after the process ends.

You're given two integers NNN and KKK.

For each i=1,2,‚Ä¶,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,‚Ä¶,N, count the number of permutations PPP of length NNN such that win(P,K)=i\text{win}(P, K) = iwin(P,K)=i.

The answer can be large, so report it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers. The iii-th of them should represent the number of permutations of {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} for which win(P,K)=i\text{win}(P, K) = iwin(P,K)=i.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 100100100.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: When N=3N = 3N=3 and K=0K = 0K=0, the winner will always be the index containing 333.
Test case 222: When N=3N = 3N=3 and K=1K = 1K=1, the last index will almost always be the winner.
The only exception is for P=[2,3,1]P = [2, 3, 1]P=[2,3,1], which proceeds as follows:

Initially, x=1x = 1x=1.
With i=2i = 2i=2, we have Pi+K=4‚Č•2=PxP_i + K = 4 \geq 2 = P_xPi‚Äč+K=4‚Č•2=Px‚Äč, so xxx gets set to iii. Now, x=2x = 2x=2.
With i=3i = 3i=3, Pi+K=2<3=PxP_i + K = 2 \lt 3 = P_xPi‚Äč+K=2<3=Px‚Äč, so xxx doesn't change.

The final value of xxx is 111, and this is the only permutation to attain this.
